title: Ditassa klotzschii
taxonomy: Ditassa klotzschii is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Rubiaceae and Loganiaceae. The order Gentianales is further divided into several suborders, with Ditassa klotzschii belonging to the suborder Gentianineae. The suborder Gentianineae is a subset of the larger clade Gentianales, which comprises over 20,000 species of flowering plants. The classification of Ditassa klotzschii is based on morphological and molecular characteristics of the plant.

intro: Ditassa klotzschii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as a type of vining shrub. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ditassa klotzschii include alkaloids, such as ditassinine, klotzschianine, and leucoditassinine, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ditassa klotzschii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and other respiratory issues.
